message:
process: redact secrets from command logs via render_command_for_log

Replace the raw `format!("{} {}", program, args.join(" "))` used to
build the log line for spawned commands in src/process.rs with a new
render_command_for_log helper that walks the argv looking for
credential-carrying flags and substitutes *** for their values before
the line is ever emitted to the ledger / stderr / JSON output.

Why this matters:

run_command_with_timeout and run_command_cancellable both write the
rendered command into the evidence log and error payloads. When
FrankenWhisper is invoked with a Hugging Face token, Anthropic key,
etc., passing the credential through as an argument — which is common
for one-shot CLI invocations — would otherwise leak the plaintext
secret into the log archive, which is committed and reviewed by
agents.

New behavior:

- render_command_for_log(program, &args) returns a string of the form
  "<program> <arg0> <arg1> …" where each arg is either kept verbatim,
  replaced wholesale with "***", or (for --flag=value forms) rewritten
  to "--flag=***".
- is_sensitive_flag matches any of:
    --hf-token / --hf_token
    --api-key / --api_key
    --access-token / --access_token
    --auth-token / --auth_token
    --password / --pass
    --secret
    --secret-key / --secret_key
- Two cases are handled:
  1. "--flag=value": the value after the '=' is replaced with "***"
     when the flag matches the sensitive list.
  2. "--flag value" (two separate argv entries): the flag name is
     left verbatim so the error is still legible, and the *next*
     argv entry is redacted to "***". redact_next carries the state
     across the loop.
- Non-sensitive flags and positional args pass through unchanged, so
  benign arguments like `--token-threshold 0.1` still appear in full
  — the substring "token" is not enough on its own to trigger
  redaction.

Both run_command_with_timeout and run_command_cancellable now call
render_command_for_log instead of the old inline join.

Test: render_command_for_log_redacts_sensitive_flags constructs an
argv containing --hf-token (two-arg form), --api-key= (inline form),
a benign --token-threshold 0.1 pair, and a positional "positional"
entry. It asserts:
  * "--hf-token ***" appears
  * "--api-key=***" appears
  * "--token-threshold 0.1" appears unchanged
  * "positional" appears unchanged
  * the literal "hf_secret_123" does NOT appear
  * the literal "secret_api_key" does NOT appear
